source.definition: complete and total, without any restriction or limitation

explanation.sourceText: The English word "absolute" comes from the Latin "absolutus," meaning "unrestricted" or "complete."
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „absolute“ provine din latină, de la „absolutus“, care înseamnă „necondiționat“ sau „complet“.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word „absolut“ also derives from the Latin „absolutus“, meaning „complete“ or „unconditional“.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „absolut“ provine tot din latină, de la „absolutus“, cu sensul de „complet“ sau „necondiționat“.

explanation.sourceText: The word 'absolute' refers to something that is unrestricted and whole.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul „absolut“ înseamnă o totalitate fără restricții.

examples.sourceText: The book is absolutely worth reading.
examples.targetText: Cartea este absolut demnă de citit.

explanation.sourceText: Used in an emotional discussion to express unwavering conviction.
explanation.targetText: Folosit pentru a exprima o convingere fermă într-o discuție emoțională.

examples.sourceText: You must absolutely believe in yourself.
examples.targetText: Trebuie să crezi absolut în tine.

examples.sourceText: That is an absolutely good idea.
He is absolutely convinced of it.
examples.targetText: El este absolut convins de asta.

learningTips.text: Think of "absolute" as something total and complete, like an absolute truth that leaves no doubt.
The Romanian word "absolut" sounds very similar to English "absolute," making it easy to remember.
Remember "absolute" by associating it with an absolute winner — the best without question!
The prefix "ab-" in "absolute" can remind you of "away" or "off," meaning something detached or unrestricted.
